mapping.a_element: asymptotic Gaussian (HAC) inference for the bridge-alpha curve
mapping.b_element: asymptotic estimates for the Levy transition densities

brief_justification: Paper A is an econometric factor-model diagnostic: it builds a capitalization-indexed bridge-alpha curve and tests it with finite-grid HAC-Gaussian functional inference and residual-block calibration on CRSP data. Paper B analytically derives an explicit distributional representation and transition-density asymptotics for Levy processes under total stochastic resetting. The only overlap is the generic idea of 'asymptotic behaviour of a stochastic quantity'; there is no role-for-role correspondence (nothing in A plays the role of the resetting mechanism, the Levy jump process, or the transition density), so the underlying machinery differs.
